text:
The Promised Day refers to the coming time of the long-awaited culmination of Father's four-hundred year plan involving Amestris, the Human Sacrifices, the Philosopher's Stone and the Nationwide Transmutation Circle. 

Overview

In order for the Promised Day to ultimately be successful, a variety of various criteria had to be achieved by Father and the Homunculi. Firstly, was the creation of Nationwide Transmutation Circle. Much like Xerxes before it, the purpose of this circle was to forcibly convert those inside the circle into a Philosopher's Stone. Like its predecessor, each central point of the Transmutation required a "crest of blood", a state of massacre and bloodshed that would soak the land in blood and sorrow of people and facilitate the activation of the circle. This step was enacted quite literally over history, either through enduring wars with rival nations or internal conflict. While the circumstances in Xerxes was simply to give Father freedom and power, the goal of the Amestris Nationwide Transmutation Circle is simply a stepping stone and means to an end.

Secondly, Father required the Human Sacrifices, five alchemists who had previously opened The Gate and survived. This was seemingly the most difficult step, as not only were alchemists who performed Human Transmutation exceedingly rare, but five were required to be alive and present on the Promised Day. This was why the State Alchemist system was created, as a way to gather and monitor skilled alchemists who had the potential to, or already had, opened The Gate. Candidates for Human Sacrifice had to not only be skilled, intelligent, and lucky, but also have the human trait of bravery; people that would choose not to run away from the danger of The Promised Day and would thus be able to be used as sacrifices.

The creation of the Homunculi were crucial to ensuring the success of The Promised Day. Their role was manifold: Not only were they (mostly) loyal, undying servants to Father, they also were made to create the circle itself, protect it from sabotage, sow discord and facilitate the crests of blood, govern and control the country so it would in be in an ample state when the day arrived, and destroy any information regarding The Promised Day itself.

Finally, The Promised Day is named so because it is a specific day, one where a solar eclipse envelops the entirety of the country of Amestris. It is heavily implied that the country itself was created for this very purpose, its founding and shaping specifically made due to being encompassed by the eclipse. Likely through his knowledge as a being from The Gate, Father was able to hypothesize that the solar eclipse was a union of the alchemical representations of the sun, which represented the male, and the moon, which represented the female. The combination and overlap of these two were seen as a "perfect being" or "God" and was thus the only time that this being would be able to be interacted with. By forcibly opening the individual Gates of the five Human Sacrifices, the resulting release of energy would be able to open the Earth's Gate, which would allow Father to forcibly "strike down God" and bring its power to Earth.

Even for a being as powerful as Father, the immense power of "God" would be too much to contain. This was the ultimate reason for the Nationwide Transmutation Circle. The energy provided by the populace of fifty million souls would act as the power source to contain the power of "God" within Father.

As The Promised Day is literally the founding reason of the country, details of the plan were secretive, but known across some individuals of Amestris. Chief among them was Central Command, who were privy to parts of the plan, but were deluded into believing that the ultimate goal was to grant immortality to a chosen few. A conspiracy of this scale was so large that it was impossible to prevent individuals from deducing parts of it, though one of the roles of the Homunculi were to silence those who discovered it before they could share their findings.

History

The Promised Day itself was first mentioned by Father during his first appearance in the manga as a toast to his faithful children before quaffing Greed's Philosopher's Stone heart back into himself and has since been mentioned sparingly and with few details. It appears, due to his excitement regarding its proximity, that General Raven and the other members of Military High Command are well aware of the time of The Promised Day, though they may not know all of the true details. From the scattered hints given by those responsible, it would seem that the completion of Sloth's underground tunnel is essential to the Promised Day, but when Alphonse Elric questions his father Van Hohenheim on what their next move should be regarding the tunnel in Chapter 82, Van responds by telling his son to look not only at the ground, but also at the sky - suggesting that Hohenheim himself knows all the details regarding Father's true plan. In Chapter 83, Ling - knowing the truth from inside Greed's soul - relays to Edward that Father plans to open The Gate when the Promised Day arrives.

Al and Van, knowing the truth, begin a contact chain from Mason to Sig and Izumi Curtis to Major Miles and Captain Buccaneer to General Grumman to 2nd Lt. Rebecca Catalina to Lt. Riza Hawkeye to Jean Havoc to Colonel Mustang, informing them all that the Promised Day - the end of this great battle - will take place in the Spring of 1915. The manga takes a short time skip to Spring 1915, when both sides gather all their resources for the final clash. In Chapter 89, as the final march begins, the younger siblings of Sergeant Denny Brosh declare that there is to be a solar eclipse on that very day, suggesting perhaps (in conjunction with Hohenheim's earlier statement) that the eclipse (and perhaps the celestial circle created by the sun's corona around the moon) is part of the ritual that will open the Gate.

Despite the combined efforts of everyone involved in its prevention, Father's plan progresses with relative smoothness as he summons the four independent Human Sacrifices (Ed, Al, Izumi and Van) and also sacrifices Pride's Philosopher's Stone to create and summon a fifth - Roy Mustang. With the five Human Sacrifices gathered around him at the center, Father enacts the final step of his plan as soon as the solar eclipse reaches its peak.

The Nationwide Transmutation Circle activates in Chapter 104, drawing in the souls of every single human being in Amestris to the center. As the Nationwide Human Transmutation occurs, the individual Gate of each Human Sacrifice is forced open, causing them to conflict with each other. This clash releases enough Human Transmutation energy to open the Gate belonging to the Earth itself. As the massive Gate opens under his control, Father expands his body to massive size with the energy of the transmutation and activates the circle in the sky created by the sun's corona around the moon which resonates with the circle on Earth to open the Gate of the Heavens, which Father calls "God". The planetary and celestial gates pull against each other and Father pulls the energy of both Gates into himself. As the dust clears, it becomes apparent that all of the roughly fifty million humans within the country - save for the Five Sacrifices and the few people who happened to be underground, near the center of the circle at the time (May Chang, Greed, Scar, King Bradley, Riza Hawkeye, Jerso, Zampano, Lan Fan and Darius) - have fallen silent and soulless. Father emerges clad in a new, perfect body - resembling young Hohenheim and housing the energy of the country's population and the power of a god.

Fortunately, a countermeasure put into place ahead of time by Van Hohenheim goes into effect in Chapter 105, activating an array which strips Father's body of all the Amestrian souls he had absorbed and allowing all of the souls to return to their rightful bodies as called back by their spirits. Scar, activating yet another countermeasure, reverses the seal Father had placed on Amestrian alchemy, giving Edward, Alphonse, Izumi and Mustang the ability to fight against Father at their full potential.

Trivia

In the anime, the center of the circle is actually much bigger than in the manga; it was able to stretch for at least three blocks worth in distance from central command. Although, as Father's pawns/co-conspirators demonstrated, one had to at least be at ground level to be safe.
Also a few soldiers were seen being swallowed in the safety of the center circle.
